mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand contraction, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output that boosts near-term earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during early-cycle recovery periods or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 3.5% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation months or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of occurrences or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of positive GDP growth quarters or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing inflation indicates supply-side resilience that prevents stagflationary pressures, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate positive returns during post-shock recovery periods or the relative growth differential loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).
